writtenAssignments.greatControversy: This passage stresses that the sanctuary and the investigative judgment are foundational truths that must be clearly understood by God's people. It declares that the sanctuary in heaven is the very centre of Christ's work for humanity, revealing the plan of salvation and the final triumph over sin. Every individual has a personal case before the judgment bar of God, making it essential for each person to study these truths for themselves.
writtenAssignments.hebrews: This passage presents Jesus as a great High Priest who has ascended to heaven. Because He has experienced human temptation yet remained without sin, He is able to fully sympathize with our weaknesses. Therefore, believers are encouraged to approach God's throne of grace with confidence, knowing they will receive mercy and find grace to help them in their time of need.
writtenAssignments.christInSanctuary: The earthly sanctuary and its services were a miniature representation of the true sanctuary in heaven where Christ ministers as our great High Priest. The daily sacrifices pointed forward to Christ's ultimate sacrifice on the cross. In the heavenly sanctuary, after His ascension, Christ entered the Most Holy Place to plead His own blood on behalf of repentant sinners and to present their prayers, perfumed with His righteousness, before the Father. This ongoing ministry assures us of His constant intercession, providing both pardon and the final cleansing from sin.

shareOptions.quote: If we confess our sins, He is faithful and just to forgive us our sins and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
And he said to me, 'For 2,300 days; then the sanctuary shall be cleansed.'
The subject of the sanctuary and the investigative judgment should be clearly understood by the people of God... The sanctuary in heaven is the very centre of Christ's work in behalf of men.

quizQuestions.question: What group of people were involved in building the original sanctuary and what were they doing at the time?
quizQuestions.answer: The Israelites, who were enslaved in Egypt. God delivered them through Moses, led them to Mount Sinai, and there instructed them to build a tabernacle.

quizQuestions.question: Why was the sanctuary constructed?
quizQuestions.answer: Exodus 25:8 – 'And let them make Me a sanctuary, that I may dwell among them.'

quizQuestions.question: Who specified the design and the materials?
quizQuestions.answer: God Himself. Exodus 25:9 – 'Make this tabernacle and all its furnishings exactly like the pattern I will show you.'

quizQuestions.question: Where did the materials come from?
quizQuestions.answer: The Israelites brought freewill offerings – gold, silver, bronze, fabrics, wood, etc. (Exodus 35:4-29). They brought so much that Moses had to tell them to stop (Exodus 36:3-6).

quizQuestions.question: Who constructed it?
quizQuestions.answer: Bezalel, Oholiab, and every skilled person to whom the Lord gave ability (Exodus 36:1-2).

quizQuestions.question: Name the three parts of the Tabernacle in the Wilderness and tell what was in each part.
quizQuestions.answer: 1. The Court – Altar of Sacrifice, Laver. 2. The Holy Place – Table of Showbread, Lampstand, Altar of Incense. 3. The Most Holy Place – Ark of the Covenant, Mercy Seat, Cherubim.

quizQuestions.question: Briefly describe each piece of furniture and tell what each represents.
quizQuestions.answer: Altar of Sacrifice: Bronze altar for burnt offerings – represents Jesus' sacrifice (Heb 10:5-10). Laver: Bronze basin for washing – represents cleansing from sin, baptism (Rev 1:4-5; Rom 6:1-4). Table of Showbread: Gold table with 12 loaves – represents Jesus, the Bread of Life (John 6:32-35). Lampstand: 7‑branched gold lamp – represents Jesus, Light of the world; oil = Holy Spirit (John 8:12; Matt 25:1-13). Altar of Incense: Gold altar for incense – represents prayers ascending and Christ's intercession (Eph 5:2; Heb 7:25). Ark of the Covenant: Gold chest with the law – represents God's presence and the Ten Commandments (Rom 10:4). Mercy Seat: Gold cover – represents meeting place with God (Heb 10:19-22). Cherubim: Gold figures – represent God's messengers (Heb 1:13-14).

quizQuestions.question: Draw a scale model of the sanctuary, the courtyard and the position of all the furnishings. Remember to add N, S, E, W.
Starting in order from inside to outside, list each of the four coverings over the sanctuary and tell what each covering represented.
quizQuestions.answer: 1. Inner royal linen (red, blue, purple, gold) – represents Jesus' character (red = sacrifice, blue = obedience, purple = royalty, gold = divinity). 2. Goats' hair (white) – represents Jesus' perfection and purity (Heb 9:12-14). 3. Rams' skins (dyed red) – represents the blood of Jesus (Isa 63:1-3). 4. Badgers' skins (plain brown) – represents Jesus covering His divinity with humanity (Isa 53:1-2).

quizQuestions.question: Memorise and discuss 1 John 1:9, Daniel 8:14, and Exodus 25:8.
quizQuestions.answer: 1 John 1:9 – confession and forgiveness. Daniel 8:14 – the 2,300‑day prophecy and cleansing of the sanctuary. Exodus 25:8 – God's desire to dwell among His people.

quizQuestions.question: The priests were from which of the 12 tribes of Israel? Why?
quizQuestions.answer: The tribe of Levi. They remained faithful when others worshipped the golden calf (Exodus 32:25-26).

quizQuestions.question: Describe the attire of the common priest.
quizQuestions.answer: Linen breeches, a plain white linen robe, a linen turban, and no sandals. All garments were woven of one piece, spotless and without blemish (Exodus 28:40-43).

quizQuestions.question: Describe the attire of the high priest.
quizQuestions.answer: In addition to the linen undergarments, he wore an embroidered linen coat, a blue robe with pomegranates and golden bells, the ephod (gold, blue, purple, scarlet) with onyx stones on the shoulders, a breastplate with 12 precious stones and the Urim and Thummim, and a gold plate on his forehead inscribed 'HOLINESS TO THE LORD' (Exodus 28:4-39).

quizQuestions.question: What kinds of animals were brought daily to the courtyard?
quizQuestions.answer: A young bull, rams, heifers, lambs, goats, and pigeons or doves – all ceremonially clean, without spot or blemish (Leviticus 1-7; John 1:29).

quizQuestions.question: Read Patriarchs and Prophets pages 343-358. Write a paragraph on how you see Christ represented in the sanctuary and its services.
quizQuestions.answer: Christ is the central figure of the sanctuary. Every sacrifice pointed to His once‑for‑all offering on the cross. The priest's ministry foreshadowed His intercession for us in the heavenly sanctuary. The furnishings – the Bread of Life, the Light of the World, the Altar of Incense (prayers) – all reveal Jesus' character and work. The cleansing of the sanctuary on the Day of Atonement prefigures the final judgment and the eradication of sin. Thus, the sanctuary is a complete gospel in symbols.
